prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `62ab2642`.
codex_response:
This task recolors the smallest orthogonally connected `0`-region to `7` and the largest to `8`, leaving the `5` scaffold unchanged. I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint because it matched the official examples; `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` had no entry for `62ab2642`.

I added [__init__.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_62ab2642/__init__.py), [verifier.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_62ab2642/verifier.py), [generator.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_62ab2642/generator.py), and [helpers.py](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_62ab2642/helpers.py). The generator explicitly builds a single acyclic border-touching `5`-tree so the zero regions look like the official family rather than generic noise. No new `arc2/dsl_ext.py` primitive was needed.

Validation passed end-to-end: official verifier `4/4`, shared build produced 1000 examples, generated-example verification passed `1000/1000`, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/62ab2642/previews/preview_01.png).
